  • Peer learning and knowledge-sharing opportunities
  • Practical exercises focused on hiring data and performance
  • A final presentation outlining key findings and recommended improvements

Throughout the program, participants will examine how hiring data is collected, managed, and used across their recruitment processes.

What Participating Cities Will Gain

By the end of the workshop, participating cities are expected to have:

  • Defined priority hiring metrics
  • Developed a better understanding of the reliability of their hiring data
  • Established baseline hiring performance
  • Identified practical improvements to strengthen data practices
  • Developed a prioritized plan to address key challenges
  • Prepared a brief for city leadership outlining findings and recommended next steps
  • Built connections with HR and operations leaders facing similar hiring challenges

The program is designed to provide participating cities with practical tools and peer support that can be applied directly to their hiring operations.

Information Sessions

Work for America will also host information sessions for individuals and cities interested in learning more about the Talent Accelerator program and the application process.

The information sessions are scheduled for:

  • July 29, 2026
  • August 26, 2026

Application Opening Date

Applications for the second Talent Accelerator cohort will open on July 27, 2026.

Cities interested in participating in the fall cohort are encouraged to learn more about the program and submit an application once the application period opens.

 APPLY HERE

Why This Opportunity Matters

Data plays an increasingly important role in improving public-sector recruitment. By helping cities better understand their hiring data, identify recruitment challenges, and develop evidence-based solutions, the Talent Accelerator program aims to support more efficient and effective hiring processes.

The program also provides an opportunity for participating cities to learn from one another, receive expert coaching, and develop practical strategies that can improve hiring outcomes.

For more Talent Accelerator program workshops, visit OFY Website HERE

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the Work for America Talent Accelerator program?

The Work for America Talent Accelerator is a free, eight-week virtual cohort program designed to help city HR and operations teams improve their hiring processes through data-driven practices.

What is the focus of the second cohort?

The second cohort will focus on “Data-Driven Hiring for Cities,” helping participating cities understand and improve how hiring data is collected, managed, and used.

When will applications open?

Applications for the second cohort will open on July 27, 2026.

How long is the program?

The Talent Accelerator program runs for eight weeks.

What activities are included in the program?

Participants will take part in weekly facilitated sessions, one-on-one coaching, peer exchange opportunities, and a final presentation focused on hiring performance findings and proposed improvements.

Are there information sessions?

Yes. Information sessions are scheduled for July 29 and August 26, 2026.

Who is the program designed for?

The program is designed for city human resources and operational leaders interested in using data and evidence to improve public-sector hiring processes.

For more information about Work for America’s Talent Accelerator program, visit their website HERE

Conclusion

Work for America’s Talent Accelerator program offers cities an opportunity to strengthen their recruitment systems, improve the use of hiring data, and learn alongside other HR and operations leaders working to address similar challenges.

With applications opening on July 27, 2026, interested cities should prepare to apply for the upcoming fall cohort and take advantage of this free professional development opportunity.

The post Work for America to Open Applications for Second Talent Accelerator Cohort appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: MENA Junior Fellowship 2026: Apply for the Third Edition of the Three-Month Research Programme
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Applications are open for the MENA Junior Fellowship 2026, a three-month research programme created for early-career scholars and independent researchers working on topics related to the Middle East and North Africa. Now entering its third edition, the fellowship aims to help emerging researchers strengthen their research skills, advance their academic development and connect with an […]

The post MENA Junior Fellowship 2026: Apply for the Third Edition of the Three-Month Research Programme appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are open for the MENA Junior Fellowship 2026, a three-month research programme created for early-career scholars and independent researchers working on topics related to the Middle East and North Africa.

Now entering its third edition, the fellowship aims to help emerging researchers strengthen their research skills, advance their academic development and connect with an international research network.

Selected fellows will receive research training, mentorship, academic supervision and publication support. Participants will also gain affiliation with a European-based research institute and access to an interdisciplinary research community.

The programme will begin on 1 September 2026. Applications must be submitted by 16 August 2026.

About the MENA Junior Fellowship 2026

The MENA Junior Fellowship is designed for researchers at the beginning of their academic or professional journeys who are conducting research on MENA-related topics.

The programme provides a structured environment in which participants can develop their research abilities while receiving guidance from experienced researchers. It also introduces fellows to an international community of scholars working across different disciplines.

The fellowship may be especially valuable for independent researchers and early-career scholars who want additional academic supervision, publication guidance or stronger connections with research institutions and scholarly networks.

Programme Duration and Start Date

The third edition of the MENA Junior Fellowship will run for three months.

The official programme start date is:

1 September 2026

Applicants should ensure that they are available to participate in the programme from the stated start date.

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship is intended for:

  • Early-career scholars
  • Independent researchers
  • Researchers working on MENA-related topics
  • Emerging researchers seeking to strengthen their academic development
  • Scholars interested in joining an international research network

Applicants should have a clear research interest connected to the Middle East and North Africa.

Main Fellowship Benefits

The updated announcement confirms that selected fellows will receive:

  • Research training
  • Mentorship
  • Academic supervision
  • Publication support
  • Affiliation with a European-based research institute
  • Access to an interdisciplinary research community
  • Connection to an international research network
  • Support for their academic development

How to Apply

Applicants should carefully review the application page for full information concerning:

  • Eligibility requirements
  • Required documents
  • Research proposal requirements
  • Selection procedures
  • Programme format
  • Attendance expectations
  • Language requirements
  • Institutional affiliation
  • Any possible costs or financial support

APPLY HERE

Important Dates

  • Application deadline: 16 August 2026

  • Programme start date: 1 September 2026

  • Programme duration: Three months

Conclusion

The MENA Junior Fellowship 2026 offers early-career scholars and independent researchers an opportunity to strengthen their research skills and connect with an international academic community.

Through research training, mentorship, academic supervision, publication support and affiliation with a European-based research institute, the programme is designed to support participants as they develop their academic trajectories.

Researchers working on topics related to the Middle East and North Africa should review the official application information and submit their applications before 16 August 2026.

The three-month programme begins on 1 September 2026.

For further information or inquiries, CLICK HERE.

To discover more fellowship opportunities, VISIT HERE.

The post MENA Junior Fellowship 2026: Apply for the Third Edition of the Three-Month Research Programme appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: GEF SGP Southern Africa Regional Workshop 2026: Apply for a Strategic Grant of Up to USD 100,000
item.dc:creator: Ning Ma
item.description:
The Global Environment Facility Small Grants Programme, implemented by the United Nations Development Programme, is inviting eligible organisations to submit proposals to host and deliver the GEF SGP Southern Africa Regional Workshop 2026. The regional workshop will take place in Harare, Zimbabwe, from 21 to 25 September 2026 and will bring together approximately 40 participants […]

The post GEF SGP Southern Africa Regional Workshop 2026: Apply for a Strategic Grant of Up to USD 100,000 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Global Environment Facility Small Grants Programme, implemented by the United Nations Development Programme, is inviting eligible organisations to submit proposals to host and deliver the GEF SGP Southern Africa Regional Workshop 2026.

The regional workshop will take place in Harare, Zimbabwe, from 21 to 25 September 2026 and will bring together approximately 40 participants connected to the Small Grants Programme across Southern Africa and the wider GEF SGP network.

The selected organisation will be responsible for coordinating the workshop, participant travel, accommodation, conferencing, field visits, exhibitions, visibility materials and documentation. The maximum proposed budget is USD 100,000, and co-financing in cash or in kind is mandatory.

Applications must be submitted by 27 July 2026 at 23:59 Central Africa Time.

About the GEF Small Grants Programme

Established in 1992, the GEF Small Grants Programme supports community-led initiatives addressing global environmental and sustainable development challenges.

The programme focuses on areas including:

  • Biodiversity conservation
  • Climate change mitigation and adaptation
  • Land degradation and sustainable land management
  • International waters
  • Chemicals and waste management

The Southern Africa Regional Workshop will create a platform for country representatives to review progress under Operational Phase 7, share lessons and good practices, discuss implementation challenges and strengthen cooperation as countries move forward with Operational Phase 8.

Workshop Participants and Countries

The workshop is expected to host 40 participants.

Represented Southern African countries include:

  • Botswana
  • Eswatini
  • Lesotho
  • Malawi
  • Mauritius
  • Mozambique
  • Namibia
  • Seychelles
  • South Africa
  • Zambia
  • Zimbabwe

Additional regional and global GEF SGP representatives may also attend.

Main Objectives of the Workshop

The workshop will enable National Coordinators and other participants to:

  1. Review progress and achievements under Operational Phase 7.
  2. Examine results, challenges and lessons from country and project-level implementation.
  3. Identify effective landscape approaches and good practices.
  4. Strengthen peer learning through project site visits.
  5. Support regional collaboration during the transition to Operational Phase 8.
  6. Produce regional knowledge materials documenting results, impacts and lessons from Southern Africa.

Responsibilities of the Selected Organisation

The successful applicant will organise and manage several major components.

Participant Travel and Allowances

The organisation will arrange return economy air tickets and applicable daily subsistence allowances for participants travelling from outside Zimbabwe.

Accommodation and Conference Facilities

Applicants must obtain at least three hotel quotations in Harare during proposal development.

The selected organisation will be expected to:

  • Book a suitable venue
  • Arrange accommodation
  • Coordinate participant check-in
  • Prepare logistical and information materials
  • Provide meals and refreshments where required
  • Support workshop facilitation and daily logistics

Project Site Visits and Exhibitions

The programme includes visits to selected GEF SGP-supported projects in Harare.

The organisation will arrange transport for participants, coordinate exhibitions and provide catering and logistical support for participating grantees.

Visibility and Information Materials

Required materials may include branded clothing, diaries, brochures, banners, gazebos, exhibition furniture and a workshop backdrop.

Documentation

The organisation must produce:

  • A workshop report
  • A video documentary covering the workshop and site visits
  • A Southern Africa publication presenting projects, OP7 results, impacts and lessons learned

The call places substantial emphasis on workshop documentation and the production of knowledge materials.

Grant Amount and Project Duration

The proposal budget must:

  • Be prepared in United States dollars
  • Not exceed USD 100,000
  • Include detailed projected activities and expenses
  • Include mandatory co-financing in cash, in kind or both

The grant period is six months, and the project must be completed by 31 December 2026.

Applicants should use the supplied GEF SGP proposal and budget templates. Although the standard generic template contains broader SGP guidance, applicants should follow the specific financial ceiling and conditions stated in this call.

Eligible Organisations

Eligible applicants must be registered organisations with the capacity to organise international workshops and produce professional knowledge products.

Eligible entities include:

  • Civil society organisations
  • National non-governmental organisations
  • Non-governmental academic institutions
  • Non-governmental educational institutions

Applicants must provide proof of registration or evidence that Private Voluntary Organisation registration is underway.

The following are not eligible:

  • Individuals
  • Private-sector companies
  • Commercial entities
  • National government institutions
  • Regional government institutions
  • Local government institutions

The applicant must demonstrate relevant experience, strong organisational capacity and a proven record aligned with the assignment.

Required Personnel and Experience

Applications must include CVs for three key personnel:

  • A project manager with at least three years of project management experience
  • A lead finance officer with at least three years of finance experience
  • A monitoring and evaluation officer with at least three years of relevant experience

Applicants should also demonstrate experience in:

  • Organising international workshops
  • Providing logistical support
  • Facilitating workshops
  • Producing publications, videos and reports
  • Managing knowledge products
  • Working on relevant GEF SGP environmental themes

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit:

  • A completed GEF SGP proposal template
  • A completed budget template
  • Proof of organisational registration
  • At least three partner or referee support letters
  • CVs of key project personnel
  • Evidence of previous publications, videos or workshop reports
  • A list of board members with certified identification copies
  • Signed minutes from the last two board meetings

Audited financial statements are considered an advantage.

Only one submission per organisation is allowed, and hard-copy applications will not be accepted. The complete application must be submitted electronically using the email address stated in the call documents.

Application Deadline

The application deadline is:

27 July 2026 at 23:59 CAT

Late proposals will not be considered. Only successful applicants will be contacted.

Conclusion

The GEF SGP Southern Africa Regional Workshop strategic grant offers an important opportunity for an experienced civil society, academic or educational organisation to manage a major regional environmental event in Harare.

The successful organisation will coordinate travel, accommodation, workshop delivery, field visits, exhibitions and professional documentation for a regional gathering of 40 participants. Applicants must demonstrate strong event-management experience, institutional capacity, environmental knowledge and the ability to deliver the assignment within a budget not exceeding USD 100,000.

Eligible organisations should complete the required proposal and budget templates and submit all supporting documents before 27 July 2026 at 23:59 CAT.

For further information and application requirements, CLICK HERE.

To download the proposal template, CLICK HERE.

To discover more grants opportunities, VISIT HERE.

The post GEF SGP Southern Africa Regional Workshop 2026: Apply for a Strategic Grant of Up to USD 100,000 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Doha Debates Ambassador Program Cohort 9 (2026): Global Leadership Opportunity for Young Changemakers Aged 18–26
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Published Date: 17 July 2026 Applications are now open for Cohort 9 of the Doha Debates Ambassador Program, a prestigious 12-week virtual leadership programme designed for young people who are passionate about improving public discourse, strengthening intercultural understanding, and driving positive change in their communities and beyond. Hosted by Doha Debates, the programme brings together […]

The post Doha Debates Ambassador Program Cohort 9 (2026): Global Leadership Opportunity for Young Changemakers Aged 18–26 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Published Date: 17 July 2026

Applications are now open for Cohort 9 of the Doha Debates Ambassador Program, a prestigious 12-week virtual leadership programme designed for young people who are passionate about improving public discourse, strengthening intercultural understanding, and driving positive change in their communities and beyond.

Hosted by Doha Debates, the programme brings together emerging leaders from around the world to participate in expert-led training, collaborative learning, mentorship, and meaningful dialogue on complex global issues. Participants develop practical leadership, communication, storytelling, systems thinking, and debate skills while building lifelong relationships with an international network of peers.

The programme is entirely virtual, making it accessible to talented young leaders worldwide who meet the eligibility requirements.

The application deadline is 24 August 2026.

About the Doha Debates Ambassador Program

The Doha Debates Ambassador Program is a unique international leadership initiative that empowers young people to become more effective communicators, bridge cultural divides, and facilitate productive conversations around today’s most pressing global challenges.

Rather than focusing solely on debate, the programme encourages participants to cultivate empathy, understand diverse perspectives, and learn how to engage in respectful dialogue across cultures and viewpoints.

Over a period of 12 weeks, selected ambassadors participate in structured learning experiences that combine:

  • Weekly live virtual sessions
  • Interactive workshops
  • Expert-led training
  • Mentorship
  • Collaborative group work
  • Personal storytelling
  • Cross-cultural dialogue
  • Practical leadership development

The programme concludes with a global virtual dialogue event known as the World Majlis, where ambassadors apply everything they have learned throughout the programme.

Why the Doha Debates Ambassador Program Was Created

Today’s global challenges require cooperation across cultures, disciplines, and societies.

Doha Debates believes that young people are essential partners in creating a more peaceful, inclusive, and collaborative world. However, meaningful collaboration requires spaces where individuals can openly exchange ideas while respecting differing opinions.

The Ambassador Program draws inspiration from the traditional Majlis, a gathering place where important issues are discussed openly and respectfully.

Within this modern virtual Majlis, participants:

  • Build trust across cultures.
  • Strengthen intercultural understanding.
  • Develop collaborative leadership skills.
  • Learn to facilitate difficult conversations.
  • Explore diverse perspectives.
  • Transform dialogue into meaningful action.

What Participants Will Learn

Throughout the programme, ambassadors receive training in a wide range of practical leadership and communication skills.

Participants will learn how to:

  • Facilitate discussions on complex global issues.
  • Participate confidently in challenging conversations.
  • Navigate intercultural communication effectively.
  • Understand diverse worldviews.
  • Clearly articulate their own perspectives.
  • Use storytelling to inspire and motivate others.
  • Build empathy across cultural differences.
  • Develop systems thinking approaches to solving global problems.
  • Foster inclusive dialogue within their own communities.
  • Engage respectfully with people holding different viewpoints.

Skills You Will Gain

By participating in the Doha Debates Ambassador Program, participants strengthen valuable lifelong skills, including:

  • Public speaking
  • Debate and dialogue
  • Active listening
  • Cross-cultural communication
  • Leadership
  • Critical thinking
  • Collaboration
  • Community engagement
  • Storytelling
  • Facilitation
  • Systems thinking
  • Global citizenship

These skills are valuable for careers in international development, diplomacy, public policy, education, advocacy, entrepreneurship, civil society, and many other fields.

Program Structure

The programme is conducted entirely online over a 12-week period.

Participants should expect to dedicate approximately:

4–5 hours per week

Activities include:

Weekly Live Sessions

  • 90-minute Zoom meetings every week.

Online Learning

Participants engage asynchronously through the Doha Debates Learning Community.

Mentorship

Participants interact with experienced mentors and facilitators throughout the programme.

Collaborative Projects

Ambassadors work together with peers from around the world.

Final World Majlis

The programme concludes with a special two-hour World Majlis, during which participants facilitate virtual dialogues using the skills they have developed.

Program Timeline

Applicants should take note of the following important dates.

Activity Date
Applications Open 1 July 2026
Application Deadline 24 August 2026
Candidate Interviews 8–11 September 2026
Acceptance Notifications 15 September 2026
Ambassador Program 22 September – 1 December 2026
World Majlis Event 8 December 2026

Benefits of the Program

Participants selected for Cohort 9 will receive numerous professional and personal development opportunities.

Benefits include:

  • Participation in a prestigious international youth leadership programme.
  • Expert-led workshops and training sessions.
  • Mentorship from experienced facilitators.
  • Development of leadership and communication skills.
  • Practical experience facilitating dialogue.
  • Cross-cultural learning experiences.
  • Networking with young leaders from around the world.
  • Access to the Doha Debates global community.
  • Opportunities to participate in future Doha Debates events.
  • Eligibility to become a mentor for future cohorts.
  • Official Certificate of Completion upon successful participation.

Who Can Apply?

The programme welcomes young leaders from around the world.

Applicants must satisfy all eligibility requirements.

Candidates must:

  • Complete the full three-step application process.
  • Be 18–26 years old when the programme begins on 22 September 2026.
  • Have reliable internet access suitable for video conferencing.
  • Be fluent in English.
  • Be available every Tuesday at 6:00 p.m. AST throughout the programme from 22 September to 1 December 2026.

How to Apply

Applications consist of three mandatory stages.

Step 1: Complete the Introduction to the Majlis Course

Applicants must first complete the Introduction to the Majlis Course within the Doha Debates Learning Community.

Successful completion earns applicants the required Majlis Master Badge.

Important:

Applicants who completed the course in previous years must complete it again for Cohort 9.

Step 2: Submit the Online Application

After earning the badge, applicants receive:

  • Official badge confirmation
  • Link to the application form

The application requires:

  • Basic personal information
  • A personal video introducing yourself
  • Reflection on the Introduction to the Majlis course

Applications cannot be submitted until the course has been completed.

Step 3: Virtual Group Interview

Shortlisted finalists will be invited to participate in a virtual group interview between:

8–11 September 2026

Successful applicants will receive acceptance notifications by:

15 September 2026

Certificate

Participants who successfully complete all programme requirements will receive an official Doha Debates Certificate.

APPLY HERE

Alumni Opportunities

Graduates of the Ambassador Program remain connected to the Doha Debates community.

Alumni may:

  • Attend future events.
  • Participate in workshops.
  • Join discussions and debates.
  • Build long-term professional networks.
  • Be invited to mentor future programme participants.

Application Deadline

The deadline for applications is:

24 August 2026

Applicants should begin the process early because completing the required Introduction to the Majlis Course is mandatory before accessing the application form.

Visit HERE to learn more about the Doha Debates Ambassador Program Cohort 9

Conclusion

The Doha Debates Ambassador Program Cohort 9 provides an outstanding opportunity for young leaders who are passionate about dialogue, leadership, intercultural communication, and global collaboration. Through expert-led training, mentorship, and engagement with peers from around the world, participants gain valuable skills that extend far beyond debate, preparing them to become effective changemakers in their communities and professions.

As a fully virtual programme, the initiative offers global accessibility while fostering meaningful international connections. Young people aged 18–26 who are eager to strengthen their leadership capacity and contribute to more constructive public discourse are encouraged to begin the application process early and submit all required materials before 24 August 2026.

For more international fellowship opportunities, visit OFY HERE

The post Doha Debates Ambassador Program Cohort 9 (2026): Global Leadership Opportunity for Young Changemakers Aged 18–26 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Digital Humanism Fellowship 2026–2027 in Austria: Fully Funded Research Opportunity for PhD Candidates and Early-Career Researchers
item.dc:creator: Richard Nyirenda
item.description:
Published Date: 17 July 2026 Applications are now open for the Digital Humanism Fellowship 2026–2027, an exciting international research opportunity for doctoral candidates and early-career postdoctoral researchers interested in exploring the relationship between technology and society. Hosted by the Institute for Human Sciences (IWM) in Vienna, Austria, the fellowship provides scholars with the opportunity to […]

The post Digital Humanism Fellowship 2026–2027 in Austria: Fully Funded Research Opportunity for PhD Candidates and Early-Career Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Published Date: 17 July 2026

Applications are now open for the Digital Humanism Fellowship 2026–2027, an exciting international research opportunity for doctoral candidates and early-career postdoctoral researchers interested in exploring the relationship between technology and society. Hosted by the Institute for Human Sciences (IWM) in Vienna, Austria, the fellowship provides scholars with the opportunity to spend three months conducting independent research while joining a vibrant interdisciplinary academic community.

The fellowship is part of the Digital Humanism Program, an initiative dedicated to understanding and shaping the complex interactions between digital technologies and humanity. Rather than focusing solely on technological innovation, the programme encourages research that critically examines how digitalization influences society, politics, economics, ethics, governance, and human rights.

Successful applicants will receive a monthly stipend, access to world-class research facilities, office space, and the opportunity to collaborate with leading scholars from diverse academic disciplines.

Applications close on 21 September 2026.

About the Digital Humanism Fellowship

The Digital Humanism Fellowship is designed to support innovative research that explores the social implications of digital technologies and contributes to creating a more human-centered digital future.

Digital Humanism recognizes that technological progress should not exist independently of human values. Instead, technology should be developed and governed in ways that strengthen democracy, protect universal human rights, promote social well-being, and improve society as a whole.

The fellowship brings together scholars from multiple disciplines to address some of the most important questions arising from rapid digital transformation.

Unlike programmes focused solely on technological innovation, the Digital Humanism Fellowship encourages applicants to examine the ethical, social, political, legal, cultural, and economic dimensions of digitalization.

What Is Digital Humanism?

Digital Humanism is an interdisciplinary research approach that seeks to:

  • Understand the relationship between technology and society.
  • Examine the societal impact of digital transformation.
  • Promote responsible technological development.
  • Ensure innovation respects universal human rights.
  • Encourage collaboration between technical and non-technical disciplines.
  • Shape digital technologies that benefit society.

The programme emphasizes that addressing the challenges of the digital age requires contributions from fields including:

  • Computer science
  • Political science
  • Sociology
  • Economics
  • Philosophy
  • Law
  • History
  • Anthropology
  • Ethics
  • Public policy
  • Media studies
  • International relations
  • Other humanities and social science disciplines

Difference Between Digital Humanism and Digital Humanities

Applicants should carefully note that this fellowship does not focus on Digital Humanities.

Projects that simply apply digital tools or technologies to humanities research are not eligible.

Instead, the fellowship seeks research that investigates broader societal questions surrounding digitalization, including:

  • Digital governance
  • Artificial intelligence and society
  • Digital democracy
  • Platform regulation
  • Data governance
  • Digital ethics
  • Technology policy
  • Privacy
  • Human rights in the digital age
  • Economic transformation
  • Geopolitical implications of technology
  • Social consequences of digitalization

Applications should clearly demonstrate how the proposed research contributes to the field of Digital Humanism.

Fellowship Duration

Successful candidates will spend:

Three consecutive months

at the Institute for Human Sciences (IWM) in Vienna.

The fellowship will take place between:

February and June 2027

The exact fellowship period will be synchronized with the residency of the Senior Digital Humanism Visiting Fellow to encourage collaboration and intellectual exchange.

Fellowships always begin on the first day of the first month and conclude on the last day of the third month.

Fellowship Benefits

The fellowship provides generous financial and institutional support.

Monthly Stipend

Successful applicants will receive:

Postdoctoral Fellows

  • EUR 3,300 per month

Applicants must have successfully defended their PhD before the application deadline.

Doctoral Candidates

  • EUR 2,750 per month

Applicants must be enrolled in a doctoral programme at the application deadline.

What the Stipend Covers

The monthly stipend is intended to help fellows cover:

  • Accommodation
  • Living expenses
  • Food
  • Local transportation
  • International travel to and from Vienna
  • Utilities
  • Internet and telecommunications
  • Health insurance
  • Research expenses
  • Literature purchases
  • Conference fees
  • Other personal expenses

Applicants should note that these costs are expected to be covered from the fellowship stipend.

Additional Benefits

Beyond the financial award, fellows receive access to numerous academic resources.

The Institute for Human Sciences provides:

  • Office space
  • High-speed internet access
  • In-house research facilities
  • Administrative support
  • Access to the IWM scholarly community
  • Invitations to seminars and academic events
  • Opportunities for interdisciplinary collaboration

These resources enable fellows to focus fully on advancing their research during the fellowship period.

Who Can Apply?

The fellowship welcomes applications from scholars across all academic disciplines, provided their proposed research aligns with the goals of Digital Humanism.

Eligible applicants include:

Doctoral Candidates

Applicants must:

  • Be enrolled in a doctoral programme at the application deadline.

Early-Career Postdoctoral Researchers

Applicants must:

  • Have obtained their PhD no more than four years before the application deadline.
  • PhD must have been awarded on or after 21 September 2022.

Additional Eligibility Requirements

Applicants should also note the following rules:

  • Only one fellowship application may be submitted during the same academic year.
  • Candidates who previously received an IWM fellowship must wait three academic years before applying again.

Failure to comply with these requirements may result in disqualification.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must prepare the following documents.

1. Letter of Motivation

The letter should explain:

  • Why the fellowship is important for the research project.
  • How the project connects with IWM’s research mission.
  • Research and writing objectives during the fellowship.

2. Short Project Description

Applicants must submit:

  • Maximum 550 characters (including spaces)

3. Full Project Proposal

Maximum:

7,500 characters (including spaces)

The proposal should include:

  • Project objectives
  • Current state of research
  • Research methodology
  • Work plan

4. Curriculum Vitae

The CV should include:

  • Educational background
  • Professional experience
  • Publications

5. Two Letters of Recommendation

Applicants must arrange:

  • Two recommendation letters from scholars familiar with their academic work.

Important:

These letters must be submitted directly by the referees before the application deadline.

After an application is submitted, referees automatically receive instructions via email.

APPLY HERE

Language Requirements

All application materials must be submitted in:

English

Applications submitted in other languages will not be considered.

Important Submission Instructions

Applicants should carefully follow the technical requirements.

Before submission:

  • Combine all application documents into one PDF file.
  • The online application system accepts only one attachment.
  • File names must contain Latin characters only.
  • Applications must be submitted through the official online application system.
  • Applications sent by email will not be accepted.

Selection Process

Applications first undergo a review to determine whether they satisfy all formal and thematic eligibility requirements.

Eligible applications are then evaluated by an expert jury.

Selection is based on:

  • Academic quality
  • Relevance to Digital Humanism
  • Research proposal
  • Overall strength of the application

Notification of Results

Applicants will receive notification of the jury’s decision by:

30 November 2026

Results will be communicated via email.

The jury:

  • Is not required to explain its decisions.
  • Will not provide individualized feedback.

Application Deadline

The deadline for submitting applications is:

21 September 2026 (23:59 CEST)

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to allow sufficient time for referees to upload recommendation letters.

Visit HERE to learn more about the Digital Humanism Fellowship 2026–2027 in Austria

Conclusion

The Digital Humanism Fellowship 2026–2027 offers an exceptional opportunity for doctoral candidates and early-career researchers seeking to explore one of the most important issues of our time the relationship between technology and society. Hosted by the Institute for Human Sciences in Vienna, the fellowship provides financial support, access to outstanding research facilities, and an intellectually stimulating environment where scholars from diverse disciplines collaborate on addressing the societal, political, ethical, and economic implications of digitalization.

With generous monthly stipends, access to a global research community, and the opportunity to spend three months conducting independent research in Austria, this fellowship is ideal for researchers committed to advancing human-centered approaches to digital transformation. Interested applicants should begin preparing their materials early, ensure all required documents are combined into a single PDF, and submit their applications before 21 September 2026.

For more international fellowship opportunities, visit OFY HERE

The post Digital Humanism Fellowship 2026–2027 in Austria: Fully Funded Research Opportunity for PhD Candidates and Early-Career Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Climate Governance, Diplomacy and Negotiations Leadership Program Call for Applications (Cohort XXI) 2026
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Climate change continues to be one of the greatest challenges facing humanity today. Across Africa, communities are increasingly experiencing the devastating impacts of rising temperatures, prolonged droughts, floods, food insecurity, biodiversity loss, and other climate-related disasters. These challenges have significant implications for livelihoods, economic growth, sustainable development, and the well-being of millions of people, especially […]

The post Climate Governance, Diplomacy and Negotiations Leadership Program Call for Applications (Cohort XXI) 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Climate change continues to be one of the greatest challenges facing humanity today. Across Africa, communities are increasingly experiencing the devastating impacts of rising temperatures, prolonged droughts, floods, food insecurity, biodiversity loss, and other climate-related disasters. These challenges have significant implications for livelihoods, economic growth, sustainable development, and the well-being of millions of people, especially women, youth, and vulnerable communities.

As the global community works together to address climate change through international agreements and negotiations, there is an increasing demand for skilled African professionals who understand climate governance, diplomacy, and international climate negotiations. Strong African representation in global climate discussions is essential to ensure that the continent’s priorities, development needs, and climate realities are effectively represented.

To strengthen Africa’s climate leadership capacity, the African Group of Negotiators Experts Support (AGNES), through the Jael Vike Climate Change Training Institute (JVCCTI), has officially opened applications for the Climate Governance, Diplomacy and Negotiations Leadership Program (CLP) Cohort XXII.

The programme is designed to equip current and emerging African climate leaders with the knowledge, leadership competencies, and practical negotiation skills required to engage effectively in regional and international climate policy processes while supporting climate action across the continent.

Applications are now open for qualified candidates.

About the Climate Governance, Diplomacy and Negotiations Leadership Program

The Climate Governance, Diplomacy and Negotiations Leadership Program (CLP) is one of AGNES’ flagship capacity-building initiatives aimed at strengthening Africa’s participation in global climate governance.

The programme is delivered through the Jael Vike Climate Change Training Institute (JVCCTI), which focuses on developing highly skilled African professionals capable of influencing climate policy, participating in international negotiations, and supporting implementation of climate agreements at national and regional levels.

As international climate negotiations become increasingly complex, African countries require a growing pool of professionals who understand the legal, scientific, political, and diplomatic dimensions of climate governance.

The programme responds to this need by preparing participants to contribute meaningfully to Africa’s climate agenda while strengthening their ability to influence climate policy both within their countries and on the international stage.

Why This Programme Matters

Climate change affects every sector of society, including agriculture, health, infrastructure, water resources, energy, education, and economic development.

Africa contributes relatively little to global greenhouse gas emissions but remains among the regions most vulnerable to climate change impacts. Consequently, effective participation in international climate negotiations is critical for ensuring that African priorities receive appropriate attention in global decision-making processes.

The programme recognizes that successful climate governance requires knowledgeable leaders who can:

  • Understand climate science
  • Develop evidence-based climate policies
  • Participate effectively in international negotiations
  • Advocate for African interests
  • Support implementation of climate agreements
  • Promote inclusive climate action involving women, youth, and marginalized communities

The Role of International Climate Agreements

The programme focuses on strengthening participants’ understanding of the major international climate frameworks that guide global climate action.

These include:

  • United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC)
  • Kyoto Protocol
  • Paris Agreement

These international agreements continue to shape global policies on:

  • Climate adaptation
  • Climate mitigation
  • Climate finance
  • Loss and damage
  • Technology transfer
  • Capacity building
  • Transparency frameworks
  • National implementation strategies

Participants will gain practical knowledge of how these agreements influence climate policy at international, regional, national, and local levels.

Programme Objectives

The overall objective of the Climate Governance, Diplomacy and Negotiations Leadership Program is to strengthen Africa’s climate leadership by building a new generation of knowledgeable and effective climate professionals.

Specifically, the programme seeks to:

  • Strengthen leadership capacity among African climate professionals
  • Improve participants’ understanding of climate governance
  • Develop advanced climate negotiation skills
  • Enhance climate diplomacy competencies
  • Increase effective African participation in international climate negotiations
  • Build the capacity of leaders to implement climate actions across Africa
  • Support emerging climate leaders, particularly women and youth

Expected Learning Outcomes

By the end of the programme, participants will have acquired both theoretical knowledge and practical skills in climate governance and international negotiations.

Participants will be able to:

1. Understand Climate Science

Participants will learn to:

  • Explain the physical science behind climate change
  • Understand the causes and impacts of climate change
  • Assess climate risks affecting Africa
  • Understand the role of the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC)
  • Interpret scientific evidence that informs climate policy

2. Develop Climate Leadership Skills

Participants will strengthen their ability to:

  • Lead climate initiatives
  • Communicate climate issues effectively
  • Engage multiple stakeholders
  • Promote inclusive climate action
  • Support collaborative decision-making

3. Understand the UNFCCC Process

Participants will gain detailed knowledge of:

  • Conference of the Parties (COP)
  • Conference of the Parties serving as the Meeting of the Parties to the Paris Agreement (CMA)
  • Conference of the Parties serving as the Meeting of the Parties to the Kyoto Protocol (CMP)
  • Subsidiary Bodies
  • Constituted Bodies
  • Negotiating Groups
  • Institutional structures of international climate governance

4. Build Practical Negotiation Skills

The programme will provide practical experience in:

  • Preparing for negotiations
  • Participating in climate negotiations
  • Reading and interpreting negotiating texts
  • Understanding negotiation brackets
  • Applying negotiation strategies
  • Supporting consensus-building during negotiations

Who Should Apply?

The programme welcomes applications from professionals working across climate-related sectors.

Eligible applicants include:

Policy and Decision Makers

Professionals responsible for designing and implementing:

  • Climate policies
  • National climate programmes
  • Local climate initiatives
  • Environmental strategies

Climate Scientists

Individuals working in:

  • Universities
  • Academic institutions
  • Think tanks
  • Climate research centres
  • Scientific organizations

Government Officials and Diplomats

Professionals involved in:

  • Climate negotiations
  • International diplomacy
  • National climate governance
  • Regional climate cooperation

Representatives of Organizations

Applicants from:

  • International organizations
  • Intergovernmental organizations
  • Non-governmental organizations (NGOs)
  • Civil society organizations
  • Youth-led organizations
  • Women-led organizations
  • Private sector institutions

working in climate change, environmental governance, and sustainable development are encouraged to apply

Legal Professionals

The programme also welcomes:

  • Lawyers
  • Climate justice advocates
  • Environmental law practitioners
  • Legal experts working in climate governance

Emerging Climate Leaders

Young professionals and early-career practitioners who wish to contribute to Africa’s climate agenda are strongly encouraged to apply.

Women and Youth Are Encouraged to Apply

The programme places particular emphasis on strengthening leadership among:

  • Women leaders
  • Youth leaders
  • Early-career professionals
  • Emerging climate practitioners

By increasing participation from these groups, the programme seeks to build a more inclusive and representative climate leadership community across Africa.

APPLY HERE

Application Requirements

Interested applicants should demonstrate:

  • Interest in climate change
  • Experience in climate policy
  • Climate governance knowledge
  • Climate diplomacy
  • Environmental governance
  • Climate science
  • Sustainable development
  • Climate negotiations
  • Related professional or academic fields

Applications should clearly show the applicant’s motivation and commitment to strengthening Africa’s climate leadership.

Application Deadline

The deadline for submitting applications is:

31 July 2026

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications well before the closing date to avoid last-minute challenges.

Visit HERE to learn more about Climate Governance, Diplomacy and Negotiations Leadership Program

Conclusion

The Climate Governance, Diplomacy and Negotiations Leadership Program Cohort XXII presents an outstanding opportunity for African professionals, policymakers, researchers, negotiators, legal experts, civil society representatives, and emerging leaders to strengthen their expertise in one of the world’s most pressing development challenges.

As climate negotiations continue to shape international policies on adaptation, mitigation, finance, and sustainable development, Africa requires highly skilled leaders who can effectively represent the continent’s interests while driving meaningful climate action at every level. Through comprehensive training in climate science, governance, diplomacy, leadership, and negotiation, the programme equips participants with the practical knowledge and strategic skills needed to contribute to stronger climate policies and successful implementation across Africa.

Professionals who are passionate about advancing climate action and strengthening Africa’s voice in global climate governance should take advantage of this opportunity and submit their applications before the deadline of 31 July 2026.

For more international career opportunities, visit OFY HERE

 

The post Climate Governance, Diplomacy and Negotiations Leadership Program Call for Applications (Cohort XXI) 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: International Anti-Corruption Academy Partial Scholarship 2026: Apply for Tuition Support to Study the Master in Anti-Corruption Studies (MACS/IMACC)
item.dc:creator: Richard Nyirenda
item.description:
Published Date: 17 July 2026 The International Anti-Corruption Academy (IACA) is offering a limited number of Partial Scholarships for students applying to its 2026 Master in Anti-Corruption Studies (MACS) or International Master in Anti-Corruption Compliance (IMACC) programmes. These scholarships are designed to support talented individuals from eligible developing countries who demonstrate both academic merit and […]

The post International Anti-Corruption Academy Partial Scholarship 2026: Apply for Tuition Support to Study the Master in Anti-Corruption Studies (MACS/IMACC) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Published Date: 17 July 2026

The International Anti-Corruption Academy (IACA) is offering a limited number of Partial Scholarships for students applying to its 2026 Master in Anti-Corruption Studies (MACS) or International Master in Anti-Corruption Compliance (IMACC) programmes. These scholarships are designed to support talented individuals from eligible developing countries who demonstrate both academic merit and financial need, while also showing strong leadership potential and a commitment to creating positive change in their communities.

The scholarship provides partial financial assistance by covering tuition fees up to EUR 17,900, helping deserving students access high-quality education in anti-corruption, compliance, governance, and ethical leadership.

Individuals who aspire to become leaders in promoting transparency, integrity, accountability, and good governance are encouraged to apply.

About the IACA Partial Scholarship

The International Anti-Corruption Academy (IACA) recognizes that financial constraints can prevent outstanding candidates from pursuing postgraduate education. To address this challenge, the Academy provides a limited number of partial scholarships each year for qualified applicants enrolling in its master’s programmes.

These scholarships are awarded based on a combination of:

  • Academic merit
  • Financial need
  • Leadership potential
  • Capacity to successfully complete the programme
  • Commitment to promoting positive change in society

Rather than simply rewarding academic excellence, the scholarship aims to identify future leaders who can make meaningful contributions in combating corruption, strengthening compliance systems, and improving governance in both the public and private sectors.

Because scholarship funding is limited, applications undergo a competitive selection process.

Scholarship Benefits

Successful applicants will receive financial support toward their postgraduate studies.

The scholarship includes:

  • Tuition fee support of up to EUR 17,900
  • Opportunity to pursue the Master in Anti-Corruption Studies (MACS) or International Master in Anti-Corruption Compliance (IMACC)
  • Access to internationally recognized education in anti-corruption and compliance
  • Opportunity to study alongside professionals from around the world
  • Exposure to global best practices in governance, ethics, transparency, and compliance

The scholarship is intended to reduce the financial burden of tuition while enabling participants to strengthen their professional expertise and leadership capacity.

What the Scholarship Does Not Cover

Applicants should note that this is a partial scholarship, meaning it does not cover all study-related expenses.

Recipients must demonstrate that they can finance:

  • Remaining tuition fees not covered by the scholarship
  • Travel expenses (where applicable)
  • Accommodation
  • Living expenses
  • Visa-related costs
  • Insurance
  • Any additional expenses not covered by the scholarship

Applicants choosing the hybrid study format must also provide evidence that they can meet any travel and participation costs required by the programme.

Who Can Apply?

The scholarship is specifically designed for applicants from eligible developing countries.

Applicants must satisfy all eligibility requirements established by IACA.

Eligible candidates must:

1. Nationality Requirement

Applicants must be citizens of:

  • Least Developed Countries (LDCs) as defined by the United Nations, or
  • IACA State Parties that are classified by the World Bank as:
    • Low-Income Economies
    • Lower-Middle-Income Economies
    • Upper-Middle-Income Economies

Only applicants from these eligible countries will be considered.

2. Admission Requirement

Applicants must have:

  • Submitted an application for admission to the Master in Anti-Corruption Studies (MACS) or International Master in Anti-Corruption Compliance (IMACC), or
  • Already been admitted into one of these programmes for the 2026 cohort.

Admission to the programme is an essential requirement before scholarship consideration.

3. Academic and Leadership Potential

Applicants must:

  • Meet all admission requirements for the master’s programme.
  • Demonstrate strong academic capability.
  • Show potential to complete the programme successfully within the two-year duration.
  • Demonstrate leadership qualities.
  • Show the ability to create positive social impact.
  • Display commitment to anti-corruption, compliance, transparency, governance, or related fields.

Scholarship Selection Criteria

Scholarships are awarded based on several factors, including:

Financial Need

Priority is given to applicants who genuinely require financial assistance to pursue their studies.

Academic Merit

Candidates should demonstrate the academic ability necessary to successfully complete the programme.

Leadership Potential

Applicants should possess strong leadership qualities and the ability to influence positive institutional and societal change.

Potential Impact

The selection committee seeks individuals who are likely to contribute significantly to anti-corruption efforts, ethical governance, and compliance initiatives after completing their studies.

Programme Completion Capacity

Candidates should demonstrate that they can complete the programme within its standard two-year duration.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should carefully complete the official scholarship application form.

Before submitting the scholarship application, applicants should ensure that they:

  • Complete every section accurately.
  • Follow all application instructions.
  • Provide truthful and complete information.
  • Upload all required supporting documents.

Incomplete applications may not be considered.

Important Application Requirement

Applicants cannot submit the scholarship application without an official IACA reference number.

This reference number is issued by the IACA Admissions Team after confirming that an applicant’s admission application is complete.

The reference number is sent via email and must be entered when completing the scholarship application form.

Applicants are therefore advised to complete their admission application early to allow sufficient time for verification and issuance of the reference number.

APPLY HERE

Why Apply for the IACA Partial Scholarship?

This scholarship offers an excellent opportunity for professionals and future leaders committed to integrity and good governance.

Benefits include:

  • Financial support toward postgraduate tuition
  • Internationally recognized master’s education
  • Specialized training in anti-corruption and compliance
  • Opportunities to strengthen leadership skills
  • Exposure to global governance best practices
  • Networking with international professionals
  • Enhanced career prospects in governance, compliance, public administration, law, development, and international organizations

Important Notes

Applicants should remember that:

  • Scholarship funding is limited.
  • Scholarships are awarded competitively.
  • Admission into the master’s programme is required before scholarship consideration.
  • Financial need alone does not guarantee selection.
  • Applicants must demonstrate leadership potential and commitment to creating positive societal impact.
  • Scholarship recipients remain responsible for expenses not covered by the award.

Application Deadline

No application deadline has been provided in the available information.

Applicants are encouraged to submit both their admission and scholarship applications as early as possible to avoid missing any institutional deadlines.

Visit HERE to learn more about International Anti-Corruption Academy Partial Scholarship

Conclusion

The IACA Partial Scholarship 2026 provides an outstanding opportunity for aspiring leaders from eligible developing countries to pursue advanced education in anti-corruption, compliance, and ethical governance. By offering tuition support of up to EUR 17,900, the scholarship helps reduce financial barriers while investing in individuals who have the potential to drive meaningful institutional and societal change.

Applicants who demonstrate academic excellence, financial need, leadership potential, and a strong commitment to integrity and transparency are strongly encouraged to apply. Since admission to the MACS or IMACC programme is a prerequisite for scholarship consideration, prospective students should begin the admissions process early and ensure they obtain their IACA reference number before submitting their scholarship application.

For more international scholarship opportunities, visit OFY HERE

The post International Anti-Corruption Academy Partial Scholarship 2026: Apply for Tuition Support to Study the Master in Anti-Corruption Studies (MACS/IMACC) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
